core_mechanism: shear-gradient-driven periodic tumbling (limit-cycle rotational dynamics) of tethered deformable elements, exhibiting non-Gaussian orientation statistics, whose collective coordination reverses the effective near-boundary transport direction and coarse-grains into an effective slip boundary condition
brief_reason: The abstract reports that individual tethered chains under shear undergo cyclic tumbling motion with non-Gaussian orientation fluctuations, and that this collective motion inverts the near-surface flow direction, describable by a Navier slip condition.
